description:
Implement Feature 00 per .specs/plan.md v1.1.

Socket.IO client with:
- Function-based auth (token provider for reconnection, reads current JWT from cookies/shared state)
- Exponential backoff on disconnect
- Event bus abstraction
- Events: message, location-update ONLY (CometChat owns typing, presence, read-receipts)
- Singleton connection in lib/socket.ts
- use-socket.ts hook for React components

Key files:
- lib/socket.ts (Socket.IO client singleton)
- lib/hooks/use-socket.ts (React hook)

Complexity: 3/5
title: Implement: WebSocket client setup (Feature 00)

description:
Integration spike per .specs/plan.md v1.1.

Build minimal group create/join/message flow to validate CometChat web SDK parity with mobile:
- Test createGroup, joinGroup, member management
- Test ban workflows
- Test 300-member threshold behavior (if possible with test data)
- Document any web SDK limitations vs React Native SDK
- Write findings to .specs/spike-cometchat-groups.md

Complexity: 2/5. Blocked by M0 completion.
notes: Spike complete: CometChat JS SDK v4.1.6 has FULL group parity with RN SDK. Same API surface for createGroup, joinGroup, member management, ban/unban, scope changes, ownership transfer. 300-member cap is platform-level (not SDK): conversation list + unread counts stop updating, but messages/typing/receipts still work. Recommended sub-region sharding for large groups. See .specs/spike-cometchat-groups.md for full report.
title: Implement: CometChat group spike (Feature 00.5a)

description:
Read .specs/analysis-consolidated.md, .specs/analysis-arch.md, and .specs/analysis-integration.md. Update .specs/plan.md addressing these critical issues:

1. REMOVE typing/presence/read-receipt from Socket.IO event list in Feature 00. CometChat owns those.
2. UPDATE Feature 03 (Auth): JWT in HTTP-only cookies, not localStorage. Add middleware.ts auth gate.
3. ADD Feature 00.5 (Integration Spikes): CometChat group spike, Geocaching API audit, Discourse category test. Insert between M0 and M1.
4. UPDATE Milestone 4 scope: ForumGateway must be BUILT from scratch (doesn't exist in backend). Increase complexity estimates.
5. UPDATE Feature 00: Socket.IO auth must use function-based token provider for reconnection.
6. UPDATE file organization: adopt route groups ((auth), (main), (legal)). Note api-client is server-side only.
7. ADD note: Mapbox requires next/dynamic with ssr:false.
8. ADD note: Discourse categories are demand-driven (5+ user threshold), not bulk WOF sync.
9. ADD note: CometChat 300-member cap affects regional rooms.
10. Commit the consolidated analysis file too.
title: Refine plan based on analysis

description: Search users by name/handle, discovery by location. Full profile view (own + others), edit, avatar upload. Requires M0. Complexity: 5/5 combined
notes:
Implementation plan for Features 13+15:

API Routes (Next.js proxy to NestJS backend at API_BASE_URL):
- GET /api/users/search?query=&limit= -> GET /users/search
- GET /api/users/[id] -> GET /users/:userId
- GET /api/users/me -> GET /users/me
- PATCH /api/users/me -> PATCH /users/me  
- POST /api/users/me/avatar -> POST /users/me/avatar

React hooks (client-side):
- use-user-search.ts - debounced search with React Query
- use-user-profile.ts - profile get/update/avatar upload

Pages:
- app/(main)/search/page.tsx - User search
- app/(main)/users/[id]/page.tsx - User profile view
- app/(main)/profile/edit/page.tsx - Edit own profile

Components:
- components/users/UserAvatar.tsx
- components/users/UserSearchBar.tsx  
- components/users/SearchResults.tsx
- components/users/UserProfileView.tsx
- components/users/EditProfileForm.tsx

Patterns: Follow existing auth route proxy pattern (getAccessToken for auth, API_BASE_URL for backend). Zustand not needed here - React Query handles server state.
title: Implement: User search + profiles (Features 13+15)

description: REST endpoints proxying Discourse API, response normalization. SSO with WOF location context. Location-aware topic suggestions. Unread counts with Redis cache. Chat-to-forum deep links. TopicCard, CategoryList, UnreadBadge components. Requires Feature 20. Complexity: 12/5 combined
notes: Analysis complete. Feature 20 (ForumGatewayService) is in backend - handles category creation. Need to build: 1) DiscourseSdk topic/notification methods 2) NestJS forum controller endpoints 3) Next.js API routes proxying to backend 4) Frontend types, hooks, components (TopicCard, CategoryList, UnreadBadge) 5) SSO URL generation 6) Deep link helpers. Patterns: Next.js API routes use cookie-based auth proxy pattern, hooks use React Query, components use Tailwind with 'use client'.
title: Implement: Discourse inline topics + SSO + activity (Features 21-25)

description:
Review .specs/plan.md for integration risks and feasibility. Focus on:
- CometChat SDK web group chat capabilities (does SDK support all mobile group features?)
- Discourse ForumGateway adapter gaps (what backend changes are actually needed for deep integration?)
- Geocaching API v1 web usage (rate limits, CORS, authentication flow)
- Privacy zone rendering on Mapbox GL JS (GeoJSON performance with many zones)
- Web push notification compatibility (Safari, Firefox, Chrome differences)
- Backend CORS/WebSocket config for web client origin
- WOF location hierarchy integration for auto-creating Discourse categories
Write analysis to .specs/analysis-integration.md
notes: Analysis complete. Written to .specs/analysis-integration.md. Key findings: CometChat web group chat feasible (300-member cap on receipts). Discourse ForumGateway needs demand-driven category creation to avoid rate limits. Geocaching API requires backend proxy (no CORS). Privacy zones render well on Mapbox GL JS. Web push has iOS Safari PWA barrier. Backend CORS/WebSocket changes are trivial. WOF category sync needs throttling and user-count thresholds.
title: Analysis: Integration & Risk review

description: Service worker registration, notification preferences, browser push API. Independent of other features. Complexity: 3/5
title: Implement: Web push notifications (Feature 27)

description:
Implement Feature 03 per .specs/plan.md v1.1.

Wire login/register to backend with JWT in HTTP-only cookies:
- JWT stored in HTTP-only cookies (NOT localStorage)
- middleware.ts auth gate for server-side redirect on unauthenticated
- Token refresh via cookie-based flow
- Login/register forms calling backend auth endpoints
- Route groups: (auth) for login/register, (main) for authenticated, (legal) for static
- CometChat SDK init after auth (read user token from cookie-based session)

Key files:
- middleware.ts (auth gate)
- app/(auth)/login/page.tsx
- app/(auth)/register/page.tsx
- app/(main)/layout.tsx (authenticated layout)
- lib/stores/auth.ts (ephemeral UI state only)

Complexity: 3/5
notes:
Implementation complete. Created 12 files:
- middleware.ts: Auth gate checking for JWT cookies, redirects to /login
- lib/auth-cookies.ts: Server-side cookie utilities (set/get/clear HTTP-only cookies)
- lib/stores/auth.ts: Zustand store for ephemeral UI auth state
- app/api/auth/login/route.ts: Proxies to backend, sets HTTP-only cookies
- app/api/auth/register/route.ts: Proxies registration, sets cookies
- app/api/auth/refresh/route.ts: Token refresh via cookie-based flow
- app/api/auth/logout/route.ts: Clears cookies and notifies backend
- app/api/auth/session/route.ts: Returns auth status for client-side checks
- app/(auth)/layout.tsx: Minimal layout for login/register (no nav bar)
- app/(auth)/login/page.tsx: Updated login using API routes for cookie auth
- app/(auth)/register/page.tsx: Updated register using API routes for cookie auth
- app/(main)/layout.tsx: Authenticated layout with session initialization

NOTE: Had to remove submodule reference for lilypad-chat-website to track files directly.
title: Implement: Auth flow completion (Feature 03)

description:

Complexity: 1/5. Blocked by M0 completion.
notes: Audit complete. Findings written to .specs/spike-geocaching-api.md. Key findings: (1) Full v1.0 API documented via Swagger — ~60 endpoints across Geocaches, Logs, Users, Friends, Trackables, Lists, Adventures, GeoTours, Waypoints. (2) Auth flow (OAuth PKCE) fully implemented in backend+website. (3) Backend proxy layer fully implemented with DTOs, exceptions, caching. (4) No staging-vs-production endpoint gaps identified. (5) Rate limits undocumented (429 status code only). (6) Feature 26 is frontend-only — uses existing backend endpoints.
title: Implement: Geocaching API audit (Feature 00.5b)

description:
Review .specs/plan.md for architecture concerns. Focus on:
- WebSocket + CometChat dual connection strategy (clear boundaries?)
- Zustand + React Query state management (right fit for Next.js app router?)
- Mock-adapter to live-API migration path (risk of breaking existing pages?)
- Mapbox GL JS integration approach (bundle size, SSR compatibility)
- Socket.IO auth token lifecycle (refresh, reconnection, race conditions)
- File organization proposed vs existing website structure
Write analysis to .specs/analysis-arch.md
notes: Analysis complete. Wrote .specs/analysis-arch.md covering all 6 requested areas plus 2 additional findings. Key concerns: (1) CometChat+Socket.IO event overlap needs single-owner resolution, (2) ForumGateway doesn't exist in backend despite roadmap marking it complete, (3) Socket.IO auth token lifecycle has reconnection race conditions. See analysis file for full details and recommendations.
title: Analysis: Architecture review

description:
Implement Feature 01 per .specs/plan.md v1.1.

Zustand stores + React Query provider + auth context:
- lib/stores/auth.ts (Zustand - ephemeral auth UI state, NOT JWT storage)
- lib/stores/chat.ts (Zustand - active conversation, message drafts)
- lib/stores/map.ts (Zustand - viewport, selected markers)
- lib/stores/ui.ts (Zustand - sidebar, modals, theme)
- React Query (TanStack Query) provider in app layout
- Auth context reading JWT from HTTP-only cookies (server-side)

Key files:
- lib/stores/*.ts
- app/providers.tsx (React Query + Zustand providers)

Complexity: 2/5
notes: Implementation complete. Created 5 files: 4 Zustand stores (auth, chat, map, ui) + React Query provider. Auth store is ephemeral UI state only (JWT lives in HTTP-only cookies). Zustand stores need no provider wrapper. React Query provider uses SSR-safe singleton pattern. Dependencies needed: zustand, @tanstack/react-query.
title: Implement: State management setup (Feature 01)

description:
Integration spike per .specs/plan.md v1.1.

Test Discourse API category operations:
- Category creation endpoint rate limits and payload requirements
- Prototype demand-driven creation with throttling (~1 req/sec)
- Test 2-level hierarchy: Country > Region/State
- Test locality tags
- Document API constraints
- Write findings to .specs/spike-discourse-categories.md

Complexity: 2/5. Blocked by M0 completion.
notes: Spike complete. Findings written to .specs/spike-discourse-categories.md. Key results: POST /categories.json supports 2-level hierarchy via parent_category_id. Default max_category_nesting=2 matches plan. Admin API rate limit 60req/min - throttled queue at ~50/min is safe. Tags via tag_groups API, restricted to categories via allowed_tag_groups. No Discourse config changes needed. 3-4 API calls per new region onboarding. No blockers for Feature 20.
title: Implement: Discourse category test (Feature 00.5c)

notes:

Need to: extend ChatMessage types with reactions field, add reaction listeners to use-messages.ts and use-groups.ts, add emoji picker + reaction pills to MessageBubble, add reaction wrappers to cometchat.ts.

Need to: define custom message types, create RichMessageCard component, extend mapMessage() to handle CustomMessage, update MessageInput with share attachment button, handle custom message rendering in both 1-to-1 and group contexts.
Key files: MessageBubble.tsx, MessageInput.tsx, use-messages.ts, use-groups.ts, cometchat.ts, types/chat.ts. New file: RichMessageCard.tsx.
title: Implement: Message reactions + rich messages (Features 09-10)

description:
Draft a formal email to Casey Vaughn at the Washington State Department of Ecology withdrawing the Shoreline Substantial Development Permit application (SSDP 24-1040) for the Wandering River Campground project.

## Context
- The LUPA appeal in Cowlitz County Superior Court is effectively dead (improper service, likely dismissed March 11)
- The project is caught between County conditions requiring guest-only access and Ecology requiring general public access — an irreconcilable conflict
- The decision is to wind down this project iteration and withdraw all pending permits
- Casey Vaughn is the Ecology contact who has been corresponding on this application

## What the email must accomplish
1. Formally request withdrawal of SSDP 24-1040
2. Request withdrawal of the Campground Binding Site Plan (BSP 24-35.01) if Ecology has jurisdiction over that
3. Politely explain the reason (project is being reconsidered / not moving forward at this time)
4. Ask for confirmation of withdrawal and any required steps
5. Professional, brief, no need to explain the full legal backstory

## Key files to reference
- /Users/jeremy/WanderingRiver/ecology-response/02_analysis/ECOLOGY_EMAIL_ANALYSIS_MARCH_2026.md — full context of the situation
- /Users/jeremy/WanderingRiver/wandering-river-lupa/05_court_filings/Draft_Email_Stipulated_Dismissal.md — tone reference

## Output
Write draft to: /Users/jeremy/WanderingRiver/ecology-response/01_correspondence/Draft_Withdrawal_Email_Ecology.md
notes: Drafted withdrawal email. Tone matches the stipulated dismissal email — professional, brief, no legal backstory. Covers: (1) formal SSDP 24-1040 withdrawal request, (2) asks about BSP 24-35.01 jurisdiction, (3) requests confirmation and next steps, (4) thanks Casey for his time.
title: Draft withdrawal email to WA Dept of Ecology (SSDP 24-1040)
